bird.name: Golden-capped parakeet
bird.summary: The golden-capped parakeet (Aratinga auricapillus) is a species of parrot in the family Psittacidae endemic to Brazil. Its natural habitats…

relations.subject: Golden-capped parakeet
relations.evidence: It is threatened by habitat loss
relations.object_summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

relations.subject: Golden-capped parakeet
relations.evidence: Though observed to be in decline due to deforestation based on data recorded in affected areas, golden-capped parakeets are more commonly found outside primary forests and have a distribution of population throughout Guyana, Brazil, and Paraguay. During a 2018 published survey of local bird species in Minas Gerais, Brazil, golden-capped parakeets received an Occurrence Frequency of "Frequent" throughout the region, and other surveys reporting sightings in the Chapada Diamantina National Park and the Serra de Ouricana have revealed that the Aratinga auricapillus' small population relatively manages to maintain its numbers.{{Cite journal|last1=Gonzaga|first1=L
relations.object_summary: 原生林被砍伐导致栖息地面积和连通性下降。

relations.subject: Golden-capped parakeet
relations.evidence: Though observed to be in decline due to deforestation based on data recorded in affected areas, golden-capped parakeets are more commonly found outside primary forests and have a distribution of population throughout Guyana, Brazil, and Paraguay. During a 2018 published survey of local bird species in Minas Gerais, Brazil, golden-capped parakeets received an Occurrence Frequency of "Frequent" throughout the region, and other surveys reporting sightings in the Chapada Diamantina National Park and the Serra de Ouricana have revealed that the Aratinga auricapillus' small population relatively manages to maintain its numbers.{{Cite journal|last1=Gonzaga|first1=L
relations.object_summary: 种群规模过小导致遗传多样性下降和脆弱性上升。
